content.thinking:
The user wants to search their local WordPress sites for content about WooCommerce or e-commerce. The skill instructions say to use the nexus CLI at the specified path. I should use the nexus search command to find this content.

Looking at the skill instructions in my system prompt (nexus-search), I should run a search command. Let me use the CLI to search for WooCommerce and e-commerce content.

The command would be:
`node /Users/jeremy.pollock/development/wpengine/local-addon-nexus-ai/bin/nexus.js content search-all "WooCommerce"`

and/or


`node /Users/jeremy.pollock/development/wpengine/local-addon-nexus-ai/bin/nexus.js content search-all "e-commerce"`

I'll run both searches in parallel to get comprehensive results.

content.text: Searching all local sites for WooCommerce and e-commerce content in parallel.

input.description: Search all local sites for WooCommerce content

content.text:
Here's what I found:

## Sites with WooCommerce / E-Commerce Content

### Most Relevant: Named Local Sites

**Faker Incorporated** вЂ” highest signal, has actual editorial content:
- **"How to Get More Out of Your WooCommerce Store"** (post) вЂ” explicitly about WooCommerce as an e-commerce platform, with advice on squeezing revenue from WooCommerce stores
- **"Services"** (page) вЂ” references digital strategy with e-commerce context
- **"Shop"** (page) вЂ” active WooCommerce shop
- **"Cart"** (page) вЂ” WooCommerce cart shortcode

**The Curated Shelf** вЂ” has a **Shop** page (WooCommerce store setup)

**faker-incorporated** / **Faker Incorporated** (appear to be two separate installs) вЂ” both have WooCommerce Shop + Cart pages

**nexustestsite1** and **nexus-test-site** вЂ” both have Shop pages (WooCommerce present)

### WPE-synced sites (many)
A large number of `wpe-*` installs have WooCommerce's standard pages (My Account, Cart) вЂ” these are real WPE environments pulled locally with WooCommerce active, but no custom e-commerce editorial content.

### Summary
The site with the most substantive e-commerce *content* is **Faker Incorporated**, which has a blog post specifically about WooCommerce strategy. The other named local sites (The Curated Shelf, nexustestsite1) have WooCommerce installed but only default shortcode pages.
